content: islink  Table of contents Prototype: islink(filename) Return type: boolean Description: Returns whether the named object filename is a symbolic link. The link node must both exist and be a symbolic link. Hard links cannot be detected using this function. Notes: islink() does not resolve symlinks as part of it's test. If a broken symlink exists, the file is still seen to be a symlink. Use filestat("myfile", "link target") to see if a file resolves to a the expected target, and check if the link target exists. Alternatively use test with returnszero() , for example returnszero("/bin/test -f myfile") . Arguments: filename : string - File object name - in the range: "?(/.*) Example: Prepare: code ln -fs /tmp/cfe_testhere.txt /tmp/link Run: code body common control { bundlesequence => { "example" }; } bundle agent example { classes : "islink" expression => islink ( "/tmp/link" ); reports : islink :: "It's a link." ; } Output: code R: It's a link. See also: fileexists() , filestat() , isdir() , isplain() , returnszero()
